title: Retoucher
description:
Remote position (only for professionals based in Argentina or Uruguay)

RYZ Labs is looking for a Pre-Press Retoucher to join one of our clientâs teams, from the sports merchandising industry. The ideal candidate will translate card designs into beautiful, error-free, and quality-checked forms that are ready for the press. This professional will support imaging, color correction, and retouching of card images/designs in line with the Pre-Press teamâs commitment to excellence.

The ideal candidate is design-savvy and organized, exhibits strong attention to detail, and brings deep experience in imaging and retouching operations. Candidates with a passion for sports and entertainment will likely find themselves a strong cultural fit.

Responsibilities:

  • Ensure all images meet color accuracy and consistency requirements
  • Maintain quality control procedures to ensure that all visual materials (e.g., images, graphics) align with the highest standards of the business and meet the specific requirements of each project
  • Work cross-functionally with photographers, designers, and other stakeholders to achieve desired retouching results for various projects
  • Identify and resolve issues related to image quality, resolution, and/or color accuracy
  • Foster a collaborative, efficient, and communication-first work environment
  • Stay informed on industry best practices, technologies, and software


Requirements:

  • Proven experience (3-5 years) in imaging and retouching operations
  • Proficiency in imaging and retouching software, such as Adobe Photoshop, Lightroom, or other specialized color correction/retouching tools
  • Strong knowledge of color theory and color management
  • Strong organizational skills, communication abilities, and attention to detail


About RYZ Labs:

RYZ Labs is a startup studio built in 2021 by two lifelong entrepreneurs. The founders of RYZ have worked at some of the world's largest tech companies and some of the most iconic consumer brands. They have lived and worked in Argentina for many years and have decades of experience in Latam. What brought them together is the passion for the early phases of company creation and the idea of attracting the brightest talents in order to build industry-defining companies in a post-pandemic world.

Our teams are remote and distributed throughout the US and Latam. They use the latest cutting-edge technologies in cloud computing to create applications that are scalable and resilient. We aim to provide diverse product solutions for different industries, planning to build a large number of startups in the upcoming years.

At RYZ, you will find yourself working with autonomy and efficiency, owning every step of your development. We provide an environment of opportunities, learning, growth, expansion and challenging projects. You will deepen your experience while sharing and learning from a team of great professionals and specialists.

Our values and what to expect:

  • Customer First Mentality - every decision we make should be made through the lens of the customer
  • Bias for Action - urgency is critical, expect that the timeline to get something done is accelerated
  • Ownership - step up if you see an opportunity to help, even if not your core responsibility
  • Humility and Respect - be willing to learn, be vulnerable, and treat everyone that interacts with RYZ with respect
  • Frugality - being frugal and cost-conscious helps us do more with less
  • Deliver Impact - get things done in the most efficient way
  • Raise our Standards - always be looking to improve our processes, our team, our expectations. Status quo is not good enough and never should be

title: Enterprise IT Support Specialist
description:
Nymbus (https://nymbus.com/) isn't just a leader in fintech; we're a community of innovators passionate about reimagining banking. Our award-winning modern core platform and cloud-based technology serve as the backbone for financial institutions eager to modernize and excel.

We operate with an AI-first mindset across our business, continuously improving how we work and the value we deliver to our clients. Our culture is grounded in collaboration, accountability, and a strong commitment to execution.

WORK ENVIRONMENT:

Nymbus is a remote-first company. Occasional travel may be required for client engagements, team planning sessions, or project activities. Working hours primarily align with the Eastern Time Zone; candidates located in the Eastern or Central time zones are preferred. While standard business hours are expected, occasional after-hours support may be required for critical operational activities.

POSITION SUMMARY:

The Enterprise IT Support Specialist provides day-to-day technical and operational support for Enterprise IT. This role is responsible for onboarding and offboarding employees, identity and access management, endpoint administration, and supporting the technologies employees rely on every day.

This is more than a traditional Help Desk role. We're looking for someone who enjoys solving problems, improving processes, and leveraging AI and automation to make Enterprise IT more efficient.

ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS/RESPONSIBILITIES:

  • Provide Tier 1 and Tier 2 technical support for employees across hardware, software, SaaS applications, and collaboration tools.
  • Identify, troubleshoot, and resolve IT-related issues.
  • Manage onboarding, offboarding, account provisioning, and access changes.
  • Administer identity and access management, including SSO, MFA, user accounts, and permissions.
  • Configure, deploy, and support macOS and Windows devices using Mobile Device Management (MDM) platforms.
  • Document, prioritize, and resolve support requests using the IT Service Management (ITSM) platform.
  • Maintain IT assets, software licensing, and operational documentation.
  • Collaborate with other IT teams to resolve technical issues and improve Enterprise IT operations.

QUALIFICATIONS:

  • 3+ years of experience in IT Support, Enterprise IT, or Systems Administration.
  • Experience supporting macOS and Windows environments.
  • Experience with identity and access management (IAM), Single Sign-On (SSO), and Mobile Device Management (MDM).
  • Experience with cloud-based SaaS applications and ITSM platforms.
  • Strong troubleshooting, communication, organization, and customer service skills.
  • Self-starter with strong ownership, curiosity, and a continuous improvement mindset.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience with JumpCloud, Google Workspace, Jamf, or similar platforms.
  • Experience with Jira Service Management.
  • Familiarity with PowerShell, Bash, Python, or other automation tools.

AI-First Expectations

AI is transforming Enterprise IT, and we expect every member of our team to embrace it as part of how they work. Successful candidates will:

  • Think AI first before approaching repetitive or manual work.
  • Use AI to improve productivity, documentation, troubleshooting, and communication.
  • Identify opportunities to automate routine tasks and improve operational efficiency.
  • Continuously learn and apply new AI capabilities responsibly and securely.

SALARY & BENEFITS:

  • Hourly pay: $38.00
  • Annual Cash Bonus and Equity Options commensurate with the role level and experience
  • 100% Remote
  • 401(k) plan
  • Insurance - Health, Dental and Vision
  • Paid Time Off

Ready to join? We invite you to watch this video and learn who we are and how we build and innovates together!

Let's Go!

title: Graphic Designer
description:
About Sezzle:

With a mission to financially empower the next generation, Sezzle is revolutionizing the shopping experience beyond payments, blending cutting-edge tech with seamless, interest-free installment plans that make shopping smarter and more accessible. Weâre not just transforming payments; weâre redefining how people discover, interact with, and purchase the things they love while driving real impact on merchant sales through increased conversions and higher order values. As we continue to shape the future of fintech and retail, weâre building an innovative, dynamic team passionate about creating more than just a transaction but a truly unique shopping journey. If youâre excited about pushing boundaries in tech and delivering a game-changing experience for consumers and merchants alike, come join us at Sezzle and help create the future of shopping!

Compensation

For this Graphic Designer, with 5+ years of experience, the compensation range is $2,500 - $3,333 USD GROSS per month as a contractor. This range is designed to accommodate early-career professionals and reflects the foundational skills and potential for growth within the role.

About the Role:

We are seeking a talented and motivated Graphic Designer who is best in class with a high IQ plus a high EQ to join our dynamic team . This role presents an exciting opportunity to thrive in a dynamic, fast-paced environment within a rapidly growing team, with abundant prospects for career advancement.

In this role, you will be instrumental in crafting visually compelling designs that resonate with our brandâs ethos and captivate our target audience. As a Graphic Designer at Sezzle, you will work closely with the marketing, product and even sales teams to produce high-quality visuals that drive engagement and elevate our brand presence across different digital platforms.

What You'll Do:

  • Visual Branding: Follow brand guidelines to develop and maintain consistent visual branding across all channels, including websites, email, print, and sales decks.
  • Creative Design: Conceptualize and design graphics for various marketing efforts, including in-app banners, emails, landing pages, sales decks, and display ads.
  • Collaboration: Work closely with the creative, marketing, and product teams to ensure goals and timelines are being met.
  • Innovation: Stay up-to-date with the latest design trends, tools, and technologies to continuously push the creative boundaries and bring fresh ideas to the table.
  • Project Management: Manage multiple design projects from concept through completion, ensuring high-quality output within deadlines.
  • Brand Storytelling: Translate complex ideas into simple, elegant visuals that tell a compelling story and enhance our brand identity.

What We Look For:

  • Experience: 5+ years of experience in graphic design, preferably within the tech or fintech industry.
  • Skills: Proficient in Figma, AI (Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, etc), Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign), and other design tools. Jira experience preferred but not required.
  • Portfolio: Strong portfolio showcasing a range of design projects, with a focus on digital campaign design.
  • Creativity: A keen eye for aesthetics, typography, and composition, with a passion for creating engaging and visually striking designs.
  • Collaboration: Excellent communication and teamwork skills, with the ability to take constructive feedback and iterate quickly.
  • Detail-Oriented: Strong attention to detail, with a commitment to delivering high-quality work.
  • Adaptability: Ability to work in a fast-paced environment, manage multiple projects, and adapt to changing priorities.
  • Education: A degree in Graphic Design, Visual Arts, or a related field is preferred, but not required if you have an exceptional portfolio and experience.
  • Demonstrated experience working with Claude or equivalent large language model tools is required; candidates must be comfortable leveraging AI to enhance productivity, research, and communication.

Why Sezzle?

  • Innovative Environment: Join a forward-thinking company that values creativity and embraces new ideas.
  • Growth Opportunities: Be part of a growing company with opportunities for professional development and career advancement.
  • Inclusive Culture: Work in an inclusive environment that fosters diversity, equity, and belonging.
  • Impactful Work: Contribute to a company that is making a positive impact on the financial future of millions.

About You:

  • You have relentlessly high standards - many people may think your standards are unreasonably high. You are continually raising the bar and driving those around you to deliver great results. You make sure that defects do not get sent down the line and that problems are fixed so they stay fixed.
  • Youâre not bound by convention - your successâand much of the funâlies in developing new ways to do things
  • You need action - speed matters in business. Many decisions and actions are reversible and do not need extensive study. We value calculated risk-taking.
  • You earn trust - you listen attentively, speak candidly, and treat others respectfully.
  • You have backbone; disagree, then commit - you can respectfully challenge decisions when you disagree, even when doing so is uncomfortable or exhausting. You have conviction and are tenacious. You do not compromise for the sake of social cohesion. Once a decision is determined, you commit wholly.
  • You deliver results - you focus on the key inputs and deliver them with the right quality and in a timely fashion. Despite setbacks, you rise to the occasion and never settle.

What Makes Working at Sezzle Awesome:

At Sezzle, we are more than just brilliant engineers, passionate data enthusiasts, out-of-the-box thinkers, and determined innovators. We believe in surrounding ourselves with only the best and the brightest individuals. Our culture is not defined by a certain set of perks designed to give the illusion of the traditional startup culture, but rather, it is the visible example living in every employee that we hire.

title: Human Resources Generalist
description:
Title: HR Generalist/Recruiter

Location: Remote (Washington State preferred)

6 month contract to hire

Pay: $30-32/hr


Required Skills & Experience


Qualifications:

- 3+ years of experience in Human Resources, Talent Acquisition, or a combined HR/Recruiting role.

- Experience managing payroll, benefits administration, and employee lifecycle processes.

- Proven full-cycle recruiting experience.

- Strong knowledge of employment laws and HR best practices.

- Experience with HRIS, payroll, and applicant tracking systems.

- Excellent communication, organizational, and relationship-building skills.

- Experience recruiting within legal, immigration, professional services, or consulting environments is preferred.


Job Description


Our client is seeking an HR Generalist & Recruiter to oversee all aspects of people operations and recruiting. This role is a 50/50 blend of Human Resources and Talent Acquisition, supporting a remote workforce while helping attract, hire, and retain top talent across legal, immigration, and business operations functions. The ideal candidate is a hands-on HR professional who is equally comfortable managing payroll and benefits as they are sourcing and recruiting candidates. This individual will serve as a trusted partner to leadership and help build scalable people processes to support continued growth.


Key Responsibilities:


Human Resources

- Administer employee benefits programs, enrollments, and employee support.

- Coordinate payroll processing and ensure accurate employee records.

- Manage workforce planning, employee onboarding, and offboarding activities.

- Maintain compliance with federal, state, and local employment laws.

- Support employee relations, performance management, and policy administration.

- Maintain HRIS and employee documentation.

- Partner with leadership on workforce management and organizational planning.


Talent Acquisition

- Lead full-cycle recruiting for legal, immigration, paralegal, attorney, case management, and corporate support positions.

- Partner with hiring managers to define hiring needs and recruitment strategies.

- Source, screen, interview, and coordinate candidates throughout the hiring process.

- Manage job postings, applicant tracking systems, and recruiting metrics. - Build talent pipelines for future hiring needs.

- Create a positive candidate experience and strengthen the employer brand.


Benefits packages for this role will start on the 1st day of employment and include medical, dental, and vision insurance, as well as HSA, FSA, and DCFSA account options, and 401k retirement account access with employer matching. Employees in this role are also entitled to paid sick leave and/or other paid time off as provided by applicable law

title: Payroll Specialist
description:
POSITION SUMMARY:

The Payroll Specialist will serve as the lead for all payroll functions.


ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:

  • Input and validate payroll on an accurate and timely basis, based on company policy and guidelines.
  • Maintains payroll information by designing systems; and collecting, calculating, and entering data.
  • Ensure systems are set up and updated to reflect our current employee base, including wages, benefits, sick and vacation time.
  • Prepare and maintain relevant management reports, including weekly, monthly, quarterly and year-end reports (i.e. gross payroll, hours worked, vacation accrual, NIB deductions, benefit deductions, etc.). Additionally, develop and prepare requested payroll reports and compensation studies for CFO, HR and President as requested.
  • Prepare all payroll-related reports. Determines payroll liabilities by calculating NIB deductions, insurance deductions, submission of workerâs compensation payments, etc.
  • Handle all Accounts Payable responsibilities related to compensation, benefit programs, incentives, rewards, etc.
  • Timely and accurately follow Corporate-directed protocols for period/year-end.
  • Maintains payroll guidelines by writing and updating policies and procedures.
  • Complies with local legal requirements by studying existing and new legislation; enforcing adherence to requirements; advising management on needed actions.
  • Administer the Companyâs Paid Time Off policies, including employee time off accruals.
  • Administer timekeeping program, including user maintenance, colleague enrollment, technical support, problem solving and manager training/support.
  • Roll-out and administer payroll compensation or incentive programs as required.
  • Assist Sales department in the administration of the Sales Consultants Compensation program.
  • Identify areas requiring process improvement and escalate to internal leadership.
  • Assist employees and managers with payroll-related questions.

title: Nurse Manager
description:
Job Details

Description

Job Summary

The Nurse Manager at Call 4 Health will oversee the daily operations of the Nurse Triage team, ensuring the highest quality of patient care, compliance with healthcare regulations, and optimal staff performance. This leadership role requires a licensed Registered Nurse (RN) with strong Operations and Triage expertise, managerial experience, and the ability to foster a supportive, high-performance work environment. This position will work closely with internal teams and directly with the customer to ensure seamless service delivery to patients.

Key Responsibilities

Operational Management:

  • Oversee the daily operations of the medical call center, ensuring efficient workflow and adherence to company policies.
  • Develop and implement operational strategies to improve service quality and patient and client satisfaction.
  • Monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) and ensure the team meets service level agreements (SLAs).
  • Collaborate with IT and technical teams to ensure seamless call center operations and troubleshoot issues.
  • Ensure appropriate call triage protocols are followed, directing patients to the proper level of care.

Staff Supervision & Leadership

  • Lead, mentor, and supervise a team of nurses and support staff.
  • Conduct staff training and professional development programs to enhance clinical and communication skills.
  • Manage staffing schedules, ensuring adequate coverage to meet call center demands.
  • Conduct performance evaluations, provide constructive feedback, and implement corrective actions when needed.
  • Foster a positive work environment that promotes teamwork, accountability, and continuous improvement.

Patient Care & Quality Assurance

  • Ensure adherence to clinical guidelines and best practices for patient care.
  • Monitor calls and interactions to assess quality, compliance, and patient satisfaction.
  • Address patient complaints, concerns, and escalations in a timely and professional manner.
  • Maintain compliance with HIPAA, state, and federal healthcare regulations.

Collaboration & Communication

  • Act as a liaison between the call center, our clients, and external stakeholders.
  • Communicate effectively with executives, medical directors, and operational teams to align strategies and goals.
  • Report on KPIs, overall performance, patient feedback, and operational efficiencies.
  • Participate in company meetings, strategy sessions, and policy development initiatives.

Education And/or Experience

  • Education: Bachelorâs Degree in Nursing (BSN) required; Masterâs in Healthcare Administration (MHA) or Nursing (MSN) preferred.
  • Licensure: Active Registered Nurse (RN) license (CPT).
  • Experience: Minimum of 5 years of Triage experience with at least 2 years in a leadership/managerial role. Experience a telehealth setting is preferred.

Qualifications & Skills

  • Strong leadership and team management abilities.
  • Excellent communication, interpersonal, and conflict resolution skills.
  • Proficiency in call center technology, electronic medical records (EMRs), and telehealth platforms.
  • Ability to analyze data, monitor performance metrics, and implement improvement strategies.
  • Strong knowledge of healthcare regulations, HIPAA compliance, and best practices in patient care.

Physical Requirements

The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodation may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

  • Ability to sit for extended periods and engage in active listening.
  • Occasional standing and use of hands for office equipment.
  • Ability to hear within normal range and use a headset.
  • Good visual acuity for reading screens and documents.
  • Ability to work remotely in a HIPAA-compliant workspace.
  • Ability to house company equipment and access broadband Internet.
  • Participation in meetings via telephone or video.

Work Environment

  • Thrive in a fast-paced environment.
  • Maintain professional relationships with diverse personalities.
  • Adaptable to change and effective in managing stress.

Benefits

  • Competitive salary and performance-based incentives.
  • Health, dental, and vision insurance.
  • Paid time off (PTO) and holidays.
  • Retirement plan options.
  • Fully Remote.

How To Apply

Interested candidates should submit their resume and a cover letter detailing their experience

And Qualifications To Call 4 Health HR Department.

Join Call 4 Health and make a meaningful impact in patient care through innovative telehealth

solutions!

Approved By

Date approved:

The Human Resource Department

February 2026

This job description may change, and/be modified with or without notice based on the department, client

and/or company needs.

2855 S. Congress Ave. | Suites A & B | Delray Beach, FL 33445

www.call4health.com

title: Strategy and Operations Lead
description:
About PatientIQ

PatientIQ is a fast-growing health tech company on a mission to improve patient outcomes. We partner with healthcare providers, medical device companies, and researchers to collect, analyze, and act on patient-reported outcomes data at scale â powering smarter clinical decisions and accelerating the future of value-based care.

Headquartered in Chicago and backed by top-tier venture capital and major health system investors, PatientIQ is building the largest platform for healthcare providers, industry partners, and researchers to collaborate on what matters most: helping patients get better.

The opportunity

PatientIQ is hiring a Strategy & Operations Lead to help drive the company's highest-priority strategic initiatives and strengthen the operating foundation required for our next phase of growth.

Working directly with the Chief of Staff and executive leadership team, you'll help build and scale our Strategic Program Office (SPO) - a new function responsible for governing the company's portfolio of strategic initiatives while improving how we plan, communicate, and execute across the business.

This role is ideal for a highly analytical operator who thrives in ambiguity, brings structure to complex problems, and enjoys turning strategy into execution.

What You'll Do

  • Own PatientIQ's portfolio of strategic initiativesâgoverning the company's highest-priority work from prioritization through execution, ensuring every initiative has clear ownership, measurable outcomes, and executive visibility
  • Lead complex cross-functional initiatives from strategy through execution, partnering across Product, Commercial, Customer Success, Engineering, Finance, and Operations to deliver measurable business results
  • Help build and scale PatientIQ's Strategic Program Office (SPO) by establishing the governance, operating cadence, portfolio management, and accountability mechanisms that enable the company to execute at scale
  • Partner with the Chief of Staff and executive leadership team on strategic planning, executive operating rhythms, organizational priorities, board and leadership communications, and other high-impact initiatives critical to the business
  • Develop executive-ready analyses, recommendations, and communications that translate complex information into clear decisions, align stakeholders, and enable faster, higher-quality execution
  • Define and measure business impact by establishing KPI frameworks, tracking strategic initiatives, surfacing risks early, and ensuring investments deliver measurable operational and commercial outcomes
  • Champion AI-enabled ways of working by standardizing AI-powered workflows, automating manual processes, and embedding AI into how teams plan, communicate, analyze, and executeâcreating meaningful leverage across the organization
  • Drive accountability across the organization by clarifying ownership, facilitating decision-making, removing execution roadblocks, and maintaining momentum on the company's most important priorities

What Success Looks Like

We believe clarity drives performance. Here's what the first 90 days look like in this role:

30 days - Build strong relationships with executive stakeholders and functional leaders. Develop a deep understanding of PatientIQ's business, product, customers, strategic priorities, and operating cadence. Begin identifying opportunities to improve execution, communication, and operational efficiency. Become proficient with the company's AI tools and workflows.

60 days - Independently own one or more executive operating cadences. Lead a cross-functional strategic initiative with minimal oversight. Deliver executive-ready analyses and communications that enable faster decision-making. Begin establishing the SPO framework

90 days - Be recognized as a trusted partner to the Chief of Staff and executive team. Fully support the company's strategic initiative portfolio and executive reporting cadence. Launch the foundational components of the Strategic Program Office. Deliver measurable improvements in execution through AI-enabled workflows and standardized operating processes.

Requirements

What you bring

Required

  • 2-4 years of experience in management consulting, investment banking, private equity, corporate strategy, or business operations at a high-growth SaaS or technology company
  • Exceptional analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to structure ambiguous questions, identify the most important issues, and develop clear, actionable recommendations
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills, including experience producing concise, executive-ready materials independently
  • Demonstrated ability to lead complex, cross-functional initiatives and drive accountability across teams without formal authority
  • Strong business judgmentâyou understand when to analyze further, when to make a recommendation, and when to push an organization toward a decision
  • Highly organized and detail-oriented, with the ability to manage multiple high-priority initiatives while maintaining a clear view of the broader business context
  • Comfort operating in a fast-moving environment where priorities evolve, information may be incomplete, and the path forward is not always defined
  • High ownership mindsetâyou do not wait for perfect direction and are comfortable building structure, defining the work, and driving it to completion
  • Strong aptitude for AI and modern productivity tools, with an interest in using technology to improve analysis, communication, and organizational efficiency
  • Exposure to go-to-market strategy, revenue operations, product strategy, healthcare technology, or B2B SaaS is a strong differentiator
  • Bachelor's degree required

Nice to have

  • Previous experience in a healthcare technology environment

Benefits

Why PatientIQ

  • Mission-driven work. We're solving a hard problem that matters â improving patient outcomes through better data. Every person here is working on something real
  • True idea meritocracy. Great ideas win, regardless of title. We encourage every team member to challenge the status quo
  • World-class team. You'll work alongside people who are at the top of their fields in health tech, enterprise software, and clinical science
  • Fast-growing. We're building the largest platform for healthcare providers and industry to collaborate â and we're just getting started
  • Great benefits. Top-notch health, dental, and vision. 401(k). Flexible PTO â we trust you to take the time you need. Professional development stipends
  • Real ownership. This is not a role where you maintain someone else's playbook. You'll build, shape strategy, and see your impact directly

title: Custodian
description:
Amentum is on the lookout for motivated Custodians to join their team in Alice Springs.

This role is responsible for cleaning and preparing vacant properties within our housing portfolio to ensure they are maintained to a professional and presentable standard.

It plays a key part in supporting property readiness by delivering thorough cleaning services that meet company requirements and expectations. Working as part of a team, custodians ensure properties are hygienic, safe, and visually appealing for incoming tenants or inspections. The role requires strong attention to detail, reliability, and teamwork, with an emphasis on quality service.

Work Schedule: Monday through Friday 8 hours per day

Essential Responsibilities:

  • Conduct assigned custodial tasks within housing portfolio
  • Mop/steam clean floors
  • Steam clean carpets, lounges, armchairs, beds and other upholstered furniture
  • Remove calcium scale build up in showers and bathrooms
  • Dusting and cobweb removal
  • Cleaning blemishes and marks on surfaces hi/lo
  • Conduct all actions to ensure highly rated customer service is provided to customer(s)
  • Maintain necessary cleaning supplies needed in provided transport (Van)
  • Maintain and store all inventory of all supplies for custodial staff.
  • Ensure, promote, and maintain health and safety practices/compliances at site and within the workplace
  • Any other reasonable duties as requested

Minimum Requirements (Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities):

  • Demonstrated experience in a similar role
  • Certificate in Health Support Services (desirable)
  • White Card (desirable)
  • Working at Heights (desirable)
  • Senior First Aid (desirable)
  • Drivers License
  • Ability to perform Medical, D&A Testing, Police Clearance and Background Checks

What We Offer

  • 13% Superannuation
  • Territory Allowance
  • Free access to 24-hour gym
  • EAP Services - which includes free confidential counselling, wellbeing services.
  • Numerous salary sacrifice options, including rent, mortgage, and novated leasing
  • Training and development to help you build specialist skills
  • Competitive salary + work life balance
  • Discounts at most Alice Springs retail outlets.
  • Education Assistance via Amentum Corporate Policy

Relocating to Alice Springs offers a lifestyle thatâs hard to match:

  • Short commutes and a calmer pace of life
  • A strong, welcoming community culture
  • Access to incredible national parks, hiking trails, and outdoor adventure
  • A setting that allows you to focus on your career while still enjoying your downtime

Work Environment, Physical Demands, and Mental Demands:

  • Typical office environment with no unusual hazards, occasional lifting to 10kgs, kneeling, standing and walking, routinely sitting and constant use of speech/hearing abilities for communication, constant mental alertness, and must be able to work under deadlines.
  • The ability to lift items up to 10kgs independently
  • The employee is frequently required to walk, sit, use hands to handle, or feel; reach with hands and arms; climb or balance; stoop, kneel, crouch, or crawl; and talk or hear. The employee is occasionally required to stand.

Security Clearance Required:

It is a condition of employment that employees obtain and retain the appropriate level of security clearance and medical clearance applicable to each role. The employee will require a NV1 Department of Defence Security Clearance (Australian)

Statement Of Work Requirements

  • All personnel assigned shall be:
  • At least 18 years of age.
  • Able to fluently read, write and speak English

This position is designated as a safety sensitive position.

As part of our commitment to maintaining a safe and compliant work environment, Amentum is a drug-free workplace and requires all personnel to comply with company drug and alcohol policies as a condition of employment.â¯ Employment is contingent upon successful completion of the drug screening process.â¯ Please note that this may include pre-hire screening for marijuana, as well as other federally controlled substances due to Amentumâs role as a federal contractor and trusted partner to the US Government.â¯â¯

Other Responsibilities:

Safety - Amentum enforces a safety culture whereby all employees have the responsibility for continuously developing and maintaining a safe work environment. As appropriate, each employee is responsible for completing all training requirements and fulfilling all self-aid/buddy aid responsibilities, participating in emergency response tasks and serving on safety committees and teams.

Quality - Quality is the foundation for the management of our business and the keystone to our goal of customer satisfaction. It is our policy to consistently provide services that meet customer expectations. Accordingly, each employee must conform to the Amentum Quality Policy and carry out job activities in compliance with applicable Amentum Quality System documents and customer contracts. Each employee must read and understand his/her Quality Management and Customer Satisfaction responsibilities.

Procedure Compliance - Each employee must read, understand and implement the general and specific operational, safety, quality and environmental requirements of all plans, procedures and policies pertaining to his/her job.

Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability or protected veteran status.

title: Manual QA Engineer
description:
Join a Company That Invests In You

Seeking Alpha is the worldâs leading community of engaged investors. Weâre the go-to destination for investors looking for actionable stock market opinions, real-time market analysis, and unique financial insights. At the same time, weâre also dedicated to creating a workplace where our team thrives. Weâre passionate about fostering a flexible, balanced environment with remote work options and an array of perks that make a real difference.

Here, your growth matters. We prioritize your development through ongoing learning and career advancement opportunities, helping you reach new milestones. Join Seeking Alpha to be part of a company that values your unique journey, supports your success, and champions both your personal well-being and professional goals.

What Weâre Looking For

We are looking for a talented and experienced Manual QA Engineer to join our QA team. In this role, you will be responsible for executing manual testing of a web and mobile application and creating and maintaining test documentation. You will work closely with product owners, developers, and customer support representatives to ensure product quality, reliability, and seamless user experience.

This role is 100% remote, offering flexibility to work from home.

The Manual QA Engineer will be engaged with the company as a contractor (Private Entrepreneur).





What You'll Do:

  • Work in close cooperation with product owners, developers, and customer support representatives
  • Create and maintain test documentation (checklists, test cases)
  • Estimate the execution time of testing activities
  • Execute manual testing of web and mobile application: functional, cross-platform, UI (responsive design)
  • Document and maintain defect reports in Jira
  • Analyze and evaluate business requirements with product owners
  • Communicate on any risks from a QA perspective that could affect feature delivery
  • Report to stakeholders on the test execution results
  • Provide support in issues investigation

Requirements:

  • 3+ years of professional experience in software QA
  • Experience in web and mobile applications testing (end-to-end, UI)
  • Strong knowledge of software QA methodologies, tools, and processes
  • Understanding of SDLC in Agile/Scrum
  • Solid knowledge of test design techniques
  • Understanding web technologies: HTML, CSS, client-server architecture, API, JavaScript
  • Good knowledge of SQL for testing purposes is a plus
  • Upper-intermediate level of English

Personal skills:

  • Good analytical skills
  • Team player with the ability to work independently
  • Self-motivated, self-disciplined, goal-driven
  • Strong attention to detail and accuracy

title: Graphic Designer
description:
Chazak Rescue is seeking a creative and passionate Graphic Designer to join our team. The Graphic Designer will be responsible for creating visually appealing content that aligns with the mission and values of Chazak Rescue. This role will work closely with the PR team, staff, and volunteers to produce graphics for various platforms, including social media, print materials, and website content. The ideal candidate will be highly creative, detail-oriented, and committed to producing quality work that supports Chazak Rescue's vision to rescue those without hope.

Main Tasks And Responsibilities

  • Design Creation: Produce engaging and high-quality designs for a wide variety of platforms, including social media, print materials, newsletters, event flyers, and promotional content.
  • Brand Consistency: Ensure all designs adhere to Chazak Rescueâs branding guidelines and visually communicate the organization's mission, vision, and core values.
  • Collaboration: Work closely with the PR team and Chazak staff to develop design concepts and ensure alignment with Chazakâs messaging.
  • Content Management: Assist in creating and managing digital content, including graphics, templates, and promotional assets for various campaigns and events.
  • Event Support: Design materials for Chazak-related events, including signage, banners, and promotional materials.
  • Design for Impact: Create designs that inspire action, promote awareness, and communicate the impact of Chazak Rescueâs work.

Qualifications

  • Proven experience in graphic design, including experience with Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign) and other design tools (Figma, Canva, etc.).
  • A portfolio that demonstrates a strong eye for design, creativity, and versatility across multiple platforms.
  • Strong attention to detail and an ability to handle multiple projects while maintaining high-quality standards.
  • Ability to work independently, prioritize tasks, and meet deadlines.
  • A commitment to learning and growing in the field of design.
  • Excellent communication skills, with the ability to collaborate with team members effectively.
  • Passion for Chazak Rescue's mission and alignment with its core values.
  • Basic photography skills are a plus.

Benefits

  • Creative Freedom: Opportunity to work on a variety of impactful and meaningful projects.
  • Collaborative Environment: Work closely with a passionate and dynamic team who is committed to making a difference.
  • Flexible Schedule: Ability to work remotely or with flexible hours.
  • Purpose-Driven Work: Contribute to a nonprofit organization with a strong commitment to helping those in need.

Compensation

We cannot offer this as an immediate paid position. There is currently no budget for this position. We are continuing our search with the understanding that a potential hire would either need to:

  • Wait to start until funding is available,
  • Raise their own support,
  • Work as a volunteer until funding is available.



We are open to making a hire with the understanding of a delayed start date.

title: Executive Assistant
description:
Executive Assistant to Founder | AI Scale-Up | Remote (London) with travel


Iâm working with an ambitious AI scale-up that is approaching an exciting stage of growth as they move towards Series A. They are looking for an experienced Executive Assistant to work directly with the Founder and CEO, becoming a trusted extension of her and helping bring structure, organisation and efficiency to a fast-paced environment.


This is a unique opportunity for someone who enjoys working closely with a founder, managing competing priorities and playing a key role in helping scale a growing technology company.


This is not a traditional EA role focused purely on administration. You will play a key role in helping the Founder operate effectively day-to-day, managing communications, relationships and priorities across investors, customers, partners and the wider team.


The role:

  • Take ownership of the Founderâs busy inbox, prioritising communications, drafting responses and ensuring important conversations are followed up
  • Manage communications on behalf of the Founder with investors, prospective investors, angels, customers, startup programmes, partners and external stakeholders
  • Build trusted relationships and ensure all interactions reflect the professionalism of the business
  • Manage and organise a complex diary, ensuring the Founderâs time is prioritised effectively
  • Prepare meeting agendas, briefing notes, presentations and supporting materials ahead of important conversations
  • Support investor meetings, customer meetings, events and speaking engagements through preparation and coordination
  • Coordinate travel, accommodation and logistics, including international travel alongside the Founder when required
  • Track actions, deadlines and follow-ups to ensure key priorities are delivered
  • Work closely with teams across product, engineering and marketing to coordinate information and create polished executive materials
  • Support company-wide meetings, leadership activities and strategic events as the business continues to grow


About you:

  • Previous experience as an Executive Assistant supporting a Founder, CEO or senior executive in a fast-paced environment
  • Strong written communication skills, with the confidence to draft messages and communicate on behalf of a senior leader
  • Excellent organisational skills with the ability to manage multiple competing priorities
  • Proactive and naturally solutions-focused, someone who anticipates what needs to happen rather than waiting for instructions
  • Comfortable handling confidential information with discretion and professionalism
  • Strong relationship-building skills with the ability to communicate confidently with senior stakeholders
  • Experience working in a start-up, scale-up or high-growth environment would be highly beneficial, but industry background is open


The ideal person will be:

  • Calm, organised and adaptable in a changing environment
  • Comfortable working closely with a founder and gaining exposure across different areas of the business
  • Confident acting as a trusted representative of the Founder
  • Someone who enjoys variety, ownership and working at pace
  • A self-starter who thrives in an entrepreneurial environment


Location & travel:

  • This is a remote-based role, offering flexibility around location. The successful candidate will need to be comfortable travelling internationally with the Founder when required, supporting meetings, events and speaking engagements.
  • The Founder has global travel planned over the coming months, so this role would suit someone who enjoys a fast-moving startup environment and the opportunity to be closely involved in the growth of the business.


Why this opportunity?

  • Youâll work directly with the Founder of a fast-growing AI company at a pivotal point in its journey, gaining exposure across investors, customers, partnerships, events and company building.
  • This is an opportunity to have a genuine impact, helping shape how a scaling technology business operates while becoming a key person the Founder relies on every day.


If this sounds like you please apply direct or send your CV to zoe.hinkinson@propellondon.com

title: Head of Business Operations
description:
About Wing:

Wing offers drone delivery as a safe, fast, and sustainable solution for last mile logistics. Consumer appetites for on-demand services are increasing, but current delivery methods are inefficient, costly, and contribute to road accidents and air pollution. Wingâs fleet of highly automated delivery drones can transport small packages directly from businesses to homes on-demand, in minutes. We design, build, and operate our aircraft, and offer drone delivery services on two continents. Our technology is designed to be easy to integrate into existing delivery and logistics networks, offering a scalable drone delivery solution for a broad range of businesses. Wing is a part of Google's parent company, Alphabet, and our mission is to create the preferred means of delivery for the planet. If you're ready to do the greatest work of your life, come join us.

About the Role:

Wing is looking for a Head of Business Operations to join our Strategy, Business Operations, and Analytics team. This role is based remotely in the United States. In this role, you will lead the Business Operations function, setting the standard for operational excellence and execution across the company. You will be responsible for building and managing a high-performing team that serves as the "connective tissue" of the organization, ensuring that high-level strategies are translated into actionable, measured, and delivered results. You will partner with executive leadership not just to plan the future, but to build the scalable systems, processes, and operating rhythms required to get us there.

What Youâll Do:

  • Lead and scale the Business Operations function, defining the teamâs charter, mentoring talent, and allocating resources to the highest-impact priorities across the company.
  • Own the company-wide operating system, including planning cycles (OKRs, annual planning), business reviews, and execution frameworks to ensure organizational alignment and velocity.
  • Translate corporate strategy into operational execution, bridging the gap between executive vision and day-to-day delivery by ensuring cross-functional teams have clear roadmaps and accountability.
  • Drive organizational efficiency and health, proactively identifying silos, operational drag, or resource imbalances, and implementing structural solutions to resolve them.
  • Serve as the primary escalation point for critical execution risks, partnering with senior-level stakeholders to unblock complex programs and drive rapid decision-making.
  • Establish the "Gold Standard" for project management and process rigor at Wing, creating toolkits and frameworks that elevate the operational maturity of the entire organization.

What Youâll Need:

  • 15+ years of experience in Business Operations or related fields, with significant experience building and leading high-performing teams.
  • A track record of "operationalizing strategy"âtaking high-level goals and building the infrastructure, processes, and teams needed to achieve them.
  • Demonstrated ability of managing a portfolio of diverse projects and stakeholders simultaneously while keeping the team focused.
  • Mastery of organizational change management, capable of guiding the company through growth phases and evolving operating models.
  • Strong executive presence with a focus on pragmatism and results; able to cut through noise to drive clarity and action among senior leadership.
  • Deep experience with resource planning, headcount management, and operational budgeting.

The US base salary range for this full-time position is the salary range below + bonus + equity + benefits. Wingâs salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for your location during the hiring process.

Salary Range: $235,000 USD - $293,000 USD

Wing is an equal opportunity employer and it is Wing's policy to comply with all applicable national, state and local laws pertaining to nondiscrimination and equal opportunity. Employment at Wing is based solely on a person's merit and qualifications directly related to professional competence. Wing does not discriminate against any employee or applicant because of race, creed, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, national origin, disability, age, genetic information, veteran status, marital status, pregnancy or related condition (including breastfeeding), or any other basis protected by law.

If you have a need that requires accommodation during the interview process due to a disability or special need, please let us know by completing our Candidate Accommodations Request Form.

title: Customer Support Agent Â· Â
description:
About Inspiration Commerce Group (ICG)

Weâre a team of ~50 people whoâve done 6 acquisitions in 18 months and scaled from $0 to >$100M in revenue. Weâre a global group with businesses across the US, UK, and EU. We buy and build companies that help people find and buy the products they love. Our portfolio includes leading fashion marketplaces like Italist, HEWI and Verishop, affiliate marketing platforms like Stylight and MyBestBrands, B2B technology solutions like Bonsai, and niche Marketplaces in the Creator and Construction spaces like Everpress and DOZR.

About This Role

Italist and HEWI are two luxury fashion marketplaces â one focused on new-season pieces sourced directly from Italian boutiques, the other on pre-loved luxury goods â and together they serve shoppers across the globe. As a Customer Support Agent, you'll be the first point of contact for our customers: answering questions, resolving issues, and making sure every interaction leaves them feeling heard and taken care of.

This is a fully remote role. You'll work closely with a small, tight-knit CS team and be expected to bring professionalism, empathy, and a real sense of ownership to every ticket you touch.

What You'll Do

  • Handle inbound customer inquiries across email and chat through Gorgias, our customer support platform â covering order status, returns, shipping, sizing, and everything in between â for either Italist or HEWI
  • Resolve issues efficiently and thoroughly, escalating to the team lead when a situation calls for it
  • Navigate Shopify and our customs admin to look up orders, track shipments, and assist with post-purchase queries across both stores
  • Maintain accurate records and follow established SOPs, while flagging anything that seems broken or improvable
  • Represent two luxury brands with warmth and professionalism in every customer interaction
  • Collaborate with teammates across time zones to ensure consistent coverage and quality

About You

  • You have hands-on experience in a customer-facing support role â e-commerce or fashion experience is a plus
  • You're calm under pressure, empathetic with frustrated customers, and quick to find solutions
  • Ownership Mentality: You don't wait to be told what to do. If a ticket needs solving, you solve it â and if something keeps going wrong, you flag it rather than work around it quietly
  • You're comfortable working independently in a remote environment with minimal hand-holding
  • Experience with Gorgias or similar helpdesk tools (Zendesk, Freshdesk) is a meaningful advantage; familiarity with Shopify is a plus
  • You're fluent in English; additional languages (Italian, French, German, Arabic, Japanise) are a genuine advantage given our customer base
  • You're okay with some structure and some ambiguity â startups are a mix of both

What We Can Offer You

  • A starting salary in the â¬1200ââ¬1,600 range (gross) per month for 40h work week + 20 days of vacation per year (compensation range varies based on candidates experience and performance during interviews)
  • A fully remote, flexible work environment â we hire adults and treat them like adults
  • Real exposure to luxury e-commerce operations across two global brands
  • A small team where your work is visible, your feedback is heard, and there's room to grow

A Note on the Contract

This is a full-time B2B engagement â meaning we'll work with you as an independent contractor rather than a direct employee. We operate this way because ICG does not currently have a legal entity in Croatia. All compensation is quoted and paid gross; you'll be responsible for your own tax and social contributions on your end. If you're already set up as a sole proprietor (e.g. pauÅ¡alni obrt or d.o.o.) or open to setting one up, this arrangement is straightforward. We're happy to walk you through how it works during the process.

What We Can't Offer You

  • A formal employment contract. This is a B2B engagement â see above.
  • Perfect work/life balance all the time. Startups move fast, and customer needs don't follow a 9-to-5 schedule. We'll work together to keep things reasonable, but flexibility goes both ways (we do not expect employees to work more then 8h a day)
  • A large support infrastructure to hide behind. You'll need to think on your feet and adapt quickly.

Our Process

  • Application â We'll review within 72 hours and follow up either way
  • Intro Call â A short conversation with Jurica or another team member to get to know each other (30 min, Google Meet).
  • Skills Assessment â A brief, practical exercise to see how you handle a realistic support scenario.
  • Final Interview â A deeper conversation with Karlo, including time for your questions.
  • Offer â The fun part.

If you have any questions at any point, feel free to reach out directly.

title: (Junior) Consultant Software-Rollout & Customer Success (w/m/d)
description:
Was sich automatisieren lässt, sollte auch automatisiert sein. Seit über 15 Jahren entwickeln wir digitale Lösungen für die Automobilbranche, die genau dafür sorgen. Unsere Plattform verbindet die unterschiedlichsten Systeme unserer Kunden und automatisiert komplexe Serviceprozesse von Anfang bis Ende. Das spart Zeit, vermeidet Fehler und macht Abläufe transparent und steuerbar – damit sich unsere Kunden aufs Wesentliche konzentrieren können.

Unsere zwei Squads haben aktuell mehr Arbeit als Zeit – und suchen ab sofort Verstärkung, die unsere Kunden beim Rollout unserer Software und darüber hinaus begleitet.

Aufgaben

Das wirst du rocken!

Du begleitest die Einführung unserer Softwarelösungen bei unseren Kunden im Automobilhandel eigenverantwortlich – vom Kick-off bis zum erfolgreichen Go-live.

  • Du steuerst die Projektphasen, behältst Timelines im Blick und bringst alle Beteiligten an einen Tisch.
  • Du arbeitest aktiv in agilen Sprints mit, nutzt Tools wie Jira und bist die Stimme des Kunden im Entwicklungsprozess.
  • Beim Customer Success bleibst du auch nach dem Go-live an deinen Kunden dran und sorgst dafür, dass sie mit unserer Software richtig durchstarten.
  • Du erkennst, wo Prozesse besser werden können – und gestaltest unsere Lösungen aktiv mit.

Qualifikation

It's a match!

Du hast ein abgeschlossenes Studium (z. B. BWL, Wirtschaftsinformatik o. ä.) oder eine Ausbildung mit relevanter Berufserfahrung.

  • Erste Erfahrung in Beratung, Projektmanagement, Customer Success oder bei Software-Rollouts ist ein Plus.
  • Du kommunizierst klar und empathisch – und fühlst dich auch auf Managementebene wohl.
  • Du arbeitest strukturiert, hast Bock auf agile Softwareentwicklung und bringst Dinge nicht nur ins Rollen, sondern auch ins Ziel.
  • Du übernimmst Verantwortung und Ownership, ohne dass man dich darum bitten muss.
  • Von Vorteil: Du kennst Aftersales- oder Serviceprozesse im Automobilhandel – aber kein Muss, den Rest bringen wir dir bei.

Benefits

Das bieten wir dir

Flexible Arbeitszeiten – ermöglicht durch klare Zielsetzungen im Arbeitsalltag.

  • Wir leben work-family-integration, deshalb kannst du einen großen Teil deiner Woche im Homeoffice arbeiten (Remote-Anteil ca. 60 %).
  • Bleib fit mit einem Zuschuss zum Fitnessstudio, Pilates, dem Jobradleasing uvm.
  • Weiterbildungsbudget und ein Umfeld, in dem du eigenverantwortlich handeln und mitgestalten kannst.
  • Modernstes Equipment (z. B. Apple) und agile Arbeitsmethoden.

Wir möchten an dieser Stelle ausdrücklich darauf hinweisen, dass du herzlich bei uns willkommen bist, ganz unabhängig von deiner Herkunft, Religion, deinem Geschlecht, Alter oder deiner sexuellen Orientierung. Auch mit Beeinträchtigungen stehen dir unsere Türen weit offen. Wir freuen uns auf ein vielfältiges Team und Miteinander bei aubex.

Für deine Bewerbung reiche deinen Lebenslauf ein oder hinterlasse uns dein LinkedIn-Profil.

title: Working Student Onpage & Content Management (f/m/d)
description:
Über Uns

Die Spread Group ist ein international agierendes Lifestyle- und Fashion-Tech-Unternehmen. Wir setzen auf einfach individualisierbare Produkte, die exakt den Vorstellungen unserer Kund*innen entsprechen. Als Vorreiter im Bereich der Made-to-Order Technologien verbinden wir Nachhaltigkeit mit passgenauen Lösungen. Ob für Privatpersonen oder Unternehmen – bei uns bekommt Fashion ihren individuellen Ausdruck. Bei uns kann Jede*r Einzigartigkeit mit individueller Fashion zum Ausdruck bringen.


Über die Rolle

Für unsere globale Fashion-Brand Spreadshirt suchen wir engagierte Talente wie dich: Bereichere als Werkstudent*in Onpage & Content Management unser dynamisches Team und lass uns gemeinsam innovative Lösungen entwickeln. Es handelt sich um eine Teilzeitposition und bietet die Flexibilität in unserem Leipziger Headquarter und remote innerhalb Deutschlands zu arbeiten. Dabei kooperierst du mit einer Vielzahl an Teams und lernst so ihre Themenfelder kennen, zum Beispiel UX, SEO, Marketing, Web Analytics und Assortment.


Was dich bei uns erwartet

  • Du kannst mitentscheiden, welche neuesten Produkte wir unseren Kund*innen zeigen und welche Geschichte wir dazu erzählen

  • Du betreust & gestaltest Themen und kleine Projekte eigenständig, zum Beispiel Kampagnen zu aktuellen Trends

  • Du bist für die On-Page Integration und Optimierung unserer Landing Pages zuständig

  • Du arbeitest mithilfe unserer Content Management Systeme an der Implementierung von neuen Texten, Verlinkungen und Bildern 

  • Du führst Qualitätsprüfungen in unseren Preview-Systemen durch


Was du mitbringen solltest

  • Du studierst im Bereich Medien- und Kommunikationswissenschaft, Medien-/Wirtschaftsinformatik, BWL oder einem anderen relevanten Bereich

  • Du bist technisch versiert und kannst Dich gut in unsere Website Nutzer*innen hineinversetzen

  • Du hast ein Auge für Details und arbeitest konzentriert und genau

  • Du hast eine hohe Lernbereitschaft und den Wunsch, mit Deinen Aufgaben zu wachsen

  • Du beherrschst Deutsch oder Englisch auf Muttersprachen-Niveau, weitere Sprachkenntnisse sind ein Plus

  • Du bringst idealerweise erste praktische Erfahrungen im Umgang mit HTML und Web Content Management Systemen mit

  • Du hast mindestens 6 Monate Zeit, uns zu unterstützen


Was wir Dir bieten

  • Flexibilität und Vertrauen: Nutze flexible Arbeitszeiten und die Möglichkeit, remote zu arbeiten. Basierend auf gegenseitigem Vertrauen findest Du so zu einer gesunden Work-Life-Balance, die Deinen persönlichen Bedürfnissen entspricht.

  • Zeit zum Auftanken: Profitiere von großzügigen 30 Urlaubstagen jährlich und genieße familienfreundliche Arbeitsbedingungen.

  • Transparenz und Wachstum: Werde Teil unserer regelmäßigen Vollversammlungen und lerne unsere gemeinsame Vision kennen. Unser umfangreiches Onboarding-Programm sowie der Besuch unserer Produktionsstätten in der Tschechischen Republik vertiefen Dein Verständnis für unsere Prozesse von Anfang an.

  • Lernen und wachsen: Erweitere Deine Skills mit Deutsch- und Englischkursen, interdisziplinären Workshops und Trainings, die Deine berufliche Entwicklung fördern.

  • Vorteile, die Freude machen: Genieße den Komfort unseres Firmenwagens und gönne Dir 50% Rabatt auf all unsere Produkte sowie exklusiven Zugang zu unwiderstehlichen Rabattaktionen.

  • Inspirierende Räume: Arbeite in modernen, inspirierenden Büros mit Dachterrasse und gemütlichen Küchen für Pausen und After-Work-Events.

  • Feiere den Erfolg: Nimm an unseren legendären Firmenfesten teil, von ausgedehnten Wandertagen bis zu unvergesslichen Sommer- und Weihnachtspartys. Freitag ist Freutag: Genieße in unserem Headquarter erfrischende Getränke, die jeden Moment unvergesslich machen.

  • Sei Du selbst: Freu Dich auf eine herzliche und offene Atmosphäre, in der Authentizität gefeiert wird und kein Dresscode Dich einschränkt. Selbstverständlich ist auch Dein vierbeiniger Freund willkommen.

  • Entdecke das Spreadster-Leben: Lerne Teammitglieder durch unser einzigartiges „Meet-a-Spreadster“-Programm kennen und gewinne schnelle Einblicke in unsere diverse (Arbeits-)Welt.

Chancengleichheit

Spread Group steht für Vielfalt. Wir sind stolz auf die stetig wachsende Diversität unseres Unternehmens und fördern sie aktiv. Dabei liegt unser Fokus auf Deinen Fähigkeiten, Talenten und Erfahrungen – unabhängig von Alter, ethnischer Zugehörigkeit, Religion, nationaler Herkunft, Geschlecht, sexueller Orientierung, Familienstand oder Behinderung.

title: Systemingenieur / Systems Engineer Embedded Software (m/w/d)
description:
Aufgaben

  • Du entwickelst Embedded Software (C/C++) für Prototypen im Bereich Steuerung und Regelung von Magnetventilen
  • Du steuerst Magnetventile und Sensorik an und entwickelst passende Algorithmen
  • Gemeinsam mit Vertrieb und F&E arbeitest du innovative Produktideen zu konkreten Konzepten aus
  • Du führst elektrische, hydraulische und mechanische Tests durch und optimierst die Systeme in enger Abstimmung mit der Hardwareentwicklung
  • Du erstellst Spezifikationen sowie Lasten- und Pflichtenhefte für die Serienentwicklung
  • Du koordinierst externe Partner und übernimmst das Anforderungsmanagement
  • Du unterstützt bei Validierungen sowie EMV-Tests und dokumentierst deine Entwicklungsarbeit und präsentierst Ergebnisse intern

Qualifikation

  • Grundverständnis von Elektronikschaltungen und Hardware-Integration
  • Erfahrung in der Ansteuerung von Aktuatoren, Sensoren oder ähnlichen Embedded-Systemen
  • Sicherer Umgang mit Mess- und Testequipment (z. B. Oszilloskop, Debugger, Labornetzteile)
  • Du kannst technische Inhalte zielgruppengerecht kommunizieren
  • Strukturierte, eigenständige Arbeitsweise und hohes Qualitätsbewusstsein
  • Erfahrung in der Zusammenarbeit mit externen Elektronikentwicklern sowie in der Beurteilung von Entwicklungsleistungen
  • Deutschkenntnisse ab Niveau C1 und Englischkenntnisse ab Niveau B2

Benefits

  • Attraktives Jahresgehalt zwischen 55.000 € und 65.000 € brutto – je nach Erfahrung und Qualifikation
  • Zentrale Rolle in einem innovativen Entwicklungsprojekt
  • Flexible Arbeitszeiten mit Gleitzeitregelung und 30 Urlaubstagen
  • Persönliche und fachliche Weiterbildungsmöglichkeiten
  • Angebote zur betrieblichen Gesundheits- und Altersvorsorge
  • Bezuschusste Mittagsmenüs in eigener Kantine mit Lounge-Terrasse
  • Verkehrsgünstige Lage mit kostenfreien Parkmöglichkeiten und sehr guter ÖPNV-Anbindung

title: Technical Product Specialist (m/w/d)
description:
Standort: 40880 Ratingen | Bereich: Systems BG - Commercial | Einstieg: Ab sofort | Anstellungsart: Vollzeit | Homeoffice: hybrid oder remote | Befristung: Nein

ÜBER ASUS

Als vielfach ausgezeichnetes Unternehmen liegt unsere Leidenschaft in der Innovation. Wir entwickeln Produkte für das digitale Leben der Gegenwart sowie Zukunft. Vom Fortune Magazin wurden wir mehrfach als eine der „World’s Most Admired Companies“ ausgezeichnet. Wir bei ASUS träumen nicht nur von Möglichkeiten. Wir entwickeln Produkte für das digitale Leben der Zukunft und suchen Talente, die unsere Vision „In Search of Incredible“ teilen und mit uns die nächste Stufe zünden.

Aufgaben

DEINE MISSION

Du verbindest technisches Know-how mit Beratungskompetenz und unterstützt unser Account Management, die Distributions- und Fachhandelspartner sowie Endkunden in Deutschland und Österreich rund um professionelle Client-Systeme. Als zentrale technische Ansprechperson für Business-Notebooks, Desktop-PCs, All-in-One-Systeme, Chromebooks und ASUS Software-Lösungen machst du komplexe Inhalte verständlich und begleitest Kundenprojekte praxisnah – von der Lösungsfindung bis zum Proof of Concept.

  • Technische Vertriebsunterstützung: Du begleitest unser lokales Account Management bei Partner- und Kundenterminen und unterstützt bei Projekten, Ausschreibungen, Angebotsanfragen und der technischen Positionierung unserer Client-Lösungen.
  • Produktpräsentationen & Lösungsberatung: Du führst technische Produktpräsentationen durch, bereitest Demos und Proof-of-Concept-Szenarien vor und übersetzt Produktvorteile in verständliche, praxisnahe Kundennutzenargumente.
  • Client-Umgebungen: Du bist erste technische Ansprechperson für BIOS / UEFI, Treiber, Softwareinstallationen, Gerätekonfiguration und Windows-Deployment. Du unterstützt bei Integration, Kompatibilitätsprüfungen, Fehleranalysen und der strukturierten Nachverfolgung technischer Fragestellungen.
  • ASUS Software Solutions: Du bist zentraler Know-how-Träger für unsere Software-Lösungen im Zusammenspiel mit ASUS Client-Systemen und berätst interne sowie externe Ansprechpartner zu Funktionen, Nutzen und Einsatzmöglichkeiten in professionellen Kundenumgebungen.
  • Training & Enablement: Du schulst Partner, Distributoren sowie interne Kolleginnen und Kollegen und bereitest technisches Produktwissen, Einsatzszenarien und Besonderheiten verständlich auf.
  • Headquarter & Marktfeedback: Du stehst in kontinuierlichem Austausch mit den FAE-Kollegen im Headquarter, überführst Produktupdates und neue Lösungsansätze in die lokale Arbeit und bündelst wiederkehrende Probleme, Marktanforderungen und Verbesserungsvorschläge.
  • Rollenfokus: Dein Beitrag ist technisch, beratend und enablement-orientiert. Du trägst keine eigene Umsatz-, Akquise- oder kommerzielle Account-Verantwortung.

Qualifikation

DEIN PROFIL

  • Qualifikation: Abgeschlossene Ausbildung oder Studium im IT-Umfeld, z. B. Fachinformatik Systemintegration oder eine vergleichbare Qualifikation.
  • Erfahrung: Berufserfahrung im IT-Helpdesk, technischen Support, Field Application Engineering oder in einer vergleichbaren technischen Rolle.
  • Client-Know-how: Gute Kenntnisse professioneller Client-Systeme, Windows-Client-Umgebungen, Gerätekonfiguration, Softwareverteilung sowie BIOS/UEFI, Treibern und typischen Fehlerbildern bei Arbeitsplatzcomputern.
  • Lösungsverständnis: Du verstehst die Integration von Geräten und Software in Unternehmensumgebungen und arbeitest dich mit Freude in neue ASUS Produkte und Software-Lösungen ein.
  • Kommunikation: Du vermittelst technische Inhalte verständlich, strukturiert und kundenorientiert und trittst bei Präsentationen, Schulungen sowie Kunden- und Partnerterminen souverän auf.
  • Sprachen: Sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse sowie gute Englischkenntnisse für die technische Abstimmung mit dem Headquarter.
  • Arbeitsweise: Du arbeitest selbstständig, strukturiert, service- und lösungsorientiert und überzeugst durch hohe technische Affinität sowie Eigeninitiative.
  • Team & Mobilität: Du arbeitest gerne mit lokalen und internationalen Teams zusammen und bringst Reisebereitschaft für Termine in Deutschland und Österreich mit.

Benefits

UNSER ANGEBOT AN DICH

  • Work-Life-Balance: Flexible Arbeitszeiten und 30 Urlaubstage für eine gute Balance zwischen Beruf und Privatleben.
  • Health & Mobility: Mit Urban Sports Club und BusinessBike unterstützen wir deine Gesundheit und nachhaltige Mobilität.
  • Vorsorge & Extras: Wir bieten dir eine betriebliche Altersvorsorge, attraktive Mitarbeiterrabatte, Corporate Benefits sowie ein Mitarbeiterempfehlungsprogramm.
  • Ausstattung: Eine moderne Mitarbeiterausstattung gehört für uns, im Büro und Mobile Office selbstverständlich dazu.
  • Culture: Eine gelebte Duz-Kultur vom Praktikanten bis zum CEO, kurze Kommunikationswege, offene Türen und hilfsbereite, engagierte Kollegen.
  • Community: Unternehmens- und Teamevents, z. B. LAN-Partys und Jahresendfeiern.
  • Impact: Hohe Eigenverantwortung und die Möglichkeit, in einem wachsenden Unternehmen viel zu bewegen.
  • Trust: Eine vertrauensvolle Position in einem internationalen Umfeld mit spannenden Aufgaben.
  • Vergütung: Dich erwartet ein transparentes Vergütungspaket bestehend aus einem Fixgehalt auf Basis von 13 Gehältern sowie einem leistungsbezogenen Bonus, der an die Erreichung definierter KPIs gekoppelt ist. Das 13. Gehalt wird jeweils zur Hälfte Ende November und Ende Dezember ausgezahlt.

BEREIT FÜR DEIN NÄCHSTES LEVEL?

Sende uns Deine aussagekräftige Bewerbung unter Angabe Deiner Gehaltsvorstellung und des frühestmöglichen Eintrittstermins ganz einfach per Bewerbungsformular.

title: Senior Manager SAP Transformation (m/w/d)
description:
Gemeinsam mit unserem Partner, einer erfolgreichen und hochspezialisierten Boutique-Beratung in Köln, suchen wir einen Senior Manager SAP Transformation (m/w/d).

Unser Partner vereint das Beste aus zwei Welten: Die Professionalität, Methodenstärke und die hochkarätigen Projekte einer klassischen Managementberatung, kombiniert mit der Agilität, Dynamik und der engen, gemeinschaftlichen Teamarbeit einer Start-up-Kultur. Hier berät ein kompaktes, schlagkräftiges Team die echten „Big Player“ der Handels- und Konsumgüterbranche bei komplexen, umfassenden Transformationen.

Wenn Du echte Wirksamkeit statt starrer Konzernstrukturen suchst, bietet Dir diese Rolle eine spannende und langfristige Perspektive mit hohem Gestaltungsspielraum.

Aufgaben

  • End-to-End Projektleitung: Du übernimmst die vollumfängliche Verantwortung für die Steuerung komplexer (SAP) Transformations- und Digitalisierungsprojekte bei namhaften Großkunden im Handels- und Konsumgüterumfeld.
  • Strategische Schnittstelle: Du agierst als zentrales Bindeglied auf C-Level- und Managementebene. Dein Fokus liegt auf der betriebswirtschaftlichen und technologischen Projektsteuerung (Budgetverantwortung, Meilensteine, Risikomanagement)
  • Ganzheitliche Führung: Du übernimmst die fachliche und disziplinarische Verantwortung für ein motiviertes Beraterteam, entwickelst Talente aktiv weiter und förderst eine progressive Kultur auf Augenhöhe.
  • Business Development & Vermarktung: Du treibst die Serviceentwicklung aktiv voran, gestaltest innovative Beratungsansätze und platzierst diese über Dein Netzwerk, auf Konferenzen oder über digitale Plattformen am Markt.
  • Strategischer Sales-Ansatz: Du begleitest den gesamten Vertriebsprozess von der ersten Kontaktaufnahme über das Pitch-Management bis hin zum erfolgreichen Projektabschluss.
  • Interne Mitgestaltung: Du bringst Dich aktiv in die Weiterentwicklung des Unternehmens ein und entwickelst progressive Ansätze aktiv mit

Qualifikation

  • Projektsteuerung: Du blickst auf nachweisbare Erfolge in der ganzheitlichen Steuerung komplexer Software-Implementierungen oder IT-Großprojekte (SAP und Non-SAP) zurück und verfügst über belastbares SAP-Verständnis (keine tief-technische Expertise benötigt)
  • Mindset: Du denkst unternehmerisch und zeichnest Dich durch einen starken vertrieblichen Antrieb ("Sales-Ansatz") aus
  • Beratungsexpertise: Du verfügst über fundierte Berufserfahrung in der IT-, Strategie- oder Managementberatung. Die Dynamik, die partnerschaftliche Kundenarbeit und die projektbezogene Leistungskultur im Consulting sind Dir vertraut
  • Leadership: Führung und Projektmanagement greifen bei Dir nahtlos ineinander. Du überzeugst durch eine starke fachliche Führungskompetenz und bringst idealerweise zudem disziplinarische Erfahrung mit
  • Branchen-Know-how: Idealerweise bringst Du Erfahrung im Handel, in der Konsumgüterindustrie oder in angrenzenden Branchen mit
  • Sprache: Du kommunizierst sicher auf Deutsch (muttersprachlerniveau) und bringst gute Englischkenntnisse mit

Benefits

  • Weiterentwicklung: Klarer Karrierepfad mit ausgezeichneten Perspektiven (2026 vom Handelsblatt für beste Karriereentwicklung in der Unternehmensberatung ausgezeichnet)
  • Maximaler Impact: Viel Gestaltungsspielraum und echte Eigenverantwortung in einer inhabergeführten Boutique-Beratung, in der Deine Leistung direkt sichtbar ist.
  • Spitzenprojekte: Inhaltlich hochspannende und umfassende Großprojekte bei echten Marktführern statt kleinteiliger Modularbeit
  • Moderne Arbeitsbedingungen: Ein flexibles Hybrid-Modell und eine moderate Reisetätigkeit (maximal 60%, im aktuellen Regelfall weniger als 2 Tage pro Woche beim Kunden).
  • Zusatzleistungen: 28 Urlaubstage plus Heiligabend & Silvester, modernste Arbeits- und Kommunikationsmittel sowie ein Zuschuss zur Urban Sports Club Mitgliedschaft.
  • Langfristigkeit & Flexibilität: Nach einer erfolgreichen Einarbeitungsphase sind individuelle Teilzeitmodelle auch für Führungskräfte abbildbar.

Klingt spannend? Dann bewirb dich jetzt! Deine Bewerbung wird selbstverständlich vertraulich behandelt. Durch die direkte Zusammenarbeit mit den Entscheidungsträgern garantieren wir Dir einen schnellen, transparenten und maßgeschneiderten Prozess.

Deine Ansprechpartnerin ist Marie-Janet.

Wir freuen uns darauf, Dich kennenzulernen!

title: Senior Typescript Developer (f/m/d)
description:
Why Join Sidestream?

At Sidestream, we’re building a team of committed, explorative and witty individuals who love pushing boundaries while having fun. Here’s what you can expect:

  • Impactful Work: Contribute to projects that matter, whether it’s digitalizing leading companies, developing open-source tool or innovating with blockchain and AI.
  • Learning & Growth: Gain mentoring from experienced professionals and access to proven processes that prepare you for success in a fast-paced tech environment.
  • Our Culture of Excellence: Embrace modern systems like the Entrepreneurial Operating System (EOS) to deliver outstanding performance and achieve ambitious goals.
  • Collaboration & Fun: Be part of a driven team that values humor, creativity and shared experiences, with regular team events and occasional meetups.
  • Flexibility: Work remotely, from our modern office in Cologne, or a mix of both—whatever helps you thrive.

Tasks

Your Responsibilities

  • Fullstack Development: Build robust applications using TypeScript, Nuxt3 and Vue.
  • Lead Projects: Collaborate with product managers to conceptualize, architect, and implement projects in agile teams.
  • Mentor and Guide: Help junior developers level up their skills through mentoring and collaborative projects.
  • Drive Innovation: Propose and implement new technologies or methodologies to keep Sidestream at the cutting edge.

Requirements

What You Bring

  • Deep Expertise: You have advanced experience in TypeScript or JavaScript and a solid understanding of frameworks like Nuxt and Vue.
  • Full-Stack Confidence: You’re comfortable working on both frontend and backend projects, taking ownership of the entire stack.
  • Leadership in Action: You can independently complete projects and enjoy mentoring less experienced developers to help them grow.
  • Craftsmanship: You take pride in writing high-quality, peer-reviewed, and production-ready code that makes a real impact.
  • Pragmatic Approach: You focus on doing the right things, the right way, efficiently and effectively.
  • Hands-On Problem Solver: You’re open to a real-life coding challenge during the application process. We value developers who enjoy rolling up their sleeves and writing great code, not just technical managers.
  • Strong Communicator: Your can communicate great in English (and German is a bonus).

No cover letter required! Instead, share 2-3 sentences about why you’re excited about this role and what makes Sidestream appealing to you. You can also include links to projects you’ve worked on to give us an impression of your skills and contributions.
